Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand User Input Domain Procure to Pay P2P process Expected Sequence 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Create Purchase Order 4 Receive Goods 5 Receive Invoice 6 Match Invoice to PO 7 Pay Invoice 8 Close Case Current Model Sequence of Create Req Approve Req PARALLEL Create PO Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice Pay Invoice Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The parallel block allows all 5 middle activities to happen in any order concurrently or even skipped depending on exact semantics but typically PARALLEL means all must happen but in any order This is underfitting because it allows invalid sequences like paying before receiving goods invoice matching before receiving invoice creating PO after receiving goods

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze Domain Logic and Constraints Create Req to Approve Req strict sequence Approve Req to Create PO strict sequence Create PO to Receive Goods strict sequence Create PO to Receive Invoice strict sequence Receive Goods and Receive Invoice to Match Invoice to PO matching requires both goods receipt and invoice and the PO Match Invoice to Pay Invoice strict sequence Pay Invoice to Close Case strict sequence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The prompt explicitly says enforce a sequence and structure that only allows the normal domain appropriate order of events and lists 1 through 8 as the standard logic

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The simplest and most correct fix is a strict SEQUENCE of all 8 activities

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: In many P2P processes Receive Goods and Receive Invoice can happen in any order relative to each other invoice might arrive before goods or vice versa but the prompt explicitly lists them as steps 4 and 5 in a specific order I should stick to the prompt stated standard logic

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify Issues with Current Model The PARALLEL operator allows create PO receive goods receive invoice match invoice and pay invoice to execute in any order This violates causal dependencies PO must be created before goods and invoice can be received Goods and invoice must be received before matching Matching must succeed before payment Underfitting The model accepts traces that are nonsensical in P2P

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Design Corrected Process Tree Replace the PARALLEL block with a SEQUENCE block containing the 5 activities in the correct order The entire tree becomes a single SEQUENCE of all 8 activities

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check prompt The fixed model should retain all the same activities but enforce a sequence and structure that only allows the normal domain appropriate order of events The prompt lists 1 through 8 explicitly A strict sequence matches this perfectly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The fix is to restrict behavior using SEQUENCE replacing PARALLEL with SEQUENCE directly solves the underfitting by restricting the language to exactly one valid trace or strictly ordered sequence which matches the domains causal dependencies
